Do I need an account to use Wispar?
My institution is not available in the list of proxies. How can it be added?
This is the official home for Wispar’s documentation.
Do I need an account to use Wispar?
Wispar has an optional cloud syncing feature to synchronize your database on multiple devices. It uses PocketBase to mirror the app's database.

Wispar has a very simple Zotero integration. If you connect your Zotero account to Wispar, you will be able to send articles directly to your Zotero account. The articles are sent into a collection of your choice. You can also create new collections and subcollections.
Wispar can forward article requests through your institution’s EZproxy, allowing access to paywalled articles without a VPN or on-premises access, provided your institution subscribes to the journal.
Wispar can be installed on Android devices in three different ways:
For iOS devices, only one installation method is available.
The OpenAlex API allows searches similar to a search engine, making boolean queries possible.
August 14, 2025 · One min read Francis Lapointe Hydrologist, Wispar developer

This is the official home for Wispar’s documentation. Here, you'll find guides, tips, and reference materials to help you get the most out of the app. From time to time, I'll also publish blog posts to share: New features Important updates Behind-the-scenes improvements Stay tuned!

No. Wispar does not require any kind of account. In fact, there is no account creation at all. Your data stays entirely in a local database on your device. An institutional account is only needed if you configure an institution in the EZproxy settings.
documents.s: Do I need an account to use Wispar?

documents.t: If your institution uses EZproxy, OpenAthens, or a similar proxy, it can be included by either submitting a pull request or opening an issue in the following GitHub repository: ezproxy-db Once the proxy is added to the repository, it will automatically become available in Wispar, since the proxy list is not cached.
documents.s: My institution is not available in the list of proxies. How can it be added?

documents.t: Wispar has an optional cloud syncing feature to synchronize your database on multiple devices. It uses PocketBase to mirror the app's database. You can either use Wispar Sync, a hosted sync service, or self-host the sync backend to stay in control of your data.

Wispar Sync is the easiest way to keep your devices in sync without managing any infrastructure. warning Before choosing this option, please take the time to read the Privacy policy. 1. Create an account Open the Wispar app and navigate to Cloud sync settings. Ensure the toggle is set to Wispar Sync (this is the default). Tap on Need an account? to switch to registration mode. Enter your email and a strong password, then tap Sign up. 2. Verify your email For security reasons, your account must be verified before you can start syncing. Check your inbox for a verification email from Wispar. Click the verification link inside the email. info If you don't receive the email, you can use the Resend email button in the app (available after a failed login attempt with an unverified account). 3. Sign in and sync Return to the login screen in Wispar. Enter your credentials and tap Login. Once authenticated, your data will begin its initial sync. You can toggle Background Sync to allow Wispar to keep your data updated automatically while you are using the app. info Syncing only happens when the app is open.

warning Do not expose your PocketBase instance to the internet unless you are aware of the risks and know what you are doing! Download PocketBase: Download the latest executable for your operating system. Launch the server: Open your terminal in the folder where you downloaded the file and run: ./pocketbase serve Initial setup: Access the dashboard at http://127.0.0.1:8090/_/ to create your initial admin account. Prepare schema: Download or copy the tables.json file from the Wispar repository. Import collections: In the PocketBase sidebar, go to Settings > Import collections. Paste the JSON content or upload the file. Ensure "Merge with existing collections" is toggled ON before clicking Import. Create your Wispar user: Navigate to the Collections menu and select the users table. Click + New record. Enter an email and password. (Note: The email doesn't need to be real unless you plan to configure mail settings later). Important: Toggle the Verified switch to ON. If this is off, Wispar will reject the login. Connect Wispar: Open the Wispar app, select Self-Hosted in the sync settings, and enter your Server URL (e.g., http://your-ip:8090). That's it!

Wispar has a very simple Zotero integration. If you connect your Zotero account to Wispar, you will be able to send articles directly to your Zotero account. The articles are sent into a collection of your choice. You can also create new collections and subcollections.

Tap the Link my Zotero account button Tap the Create a new API key button Sign in to your Zotero account. You will then be redirected to the Zotero API key generation page. Start by typing a name for your API key. Next, check both "Allow library access" and "Allow write access". Without write access, Wispar will not be able to send articles into your account. Leave the group permissions to their default values or select "None". You can leave the specific groups permissions unchecked if you are not going to send articles to group collections. Otherwise, please select "Read/Write" permisions. Tap on the Save button. Your API key will be revealed. You are almost done! Copy your API key and return to Wispar. Paste your API key into the text field. Tap on the "Save" button. Tip If you see a successful message, you are all done! If an error message was shown, please verify your API key. Also double check you have set the correct permissions when creating the key.

If you have already completed on-boarding, you can still link your Zotero account through the app settings. From the Home screen: Tap the Wispar icon at the top-right corner and select Settings. Tap the Zotero tile and follow the steps in Method 1 from step 2.

Wispar can forward article requests through your institution’s EZproxy, allowing access to paywalled articles without a VPN or on-premises access, provided your institution subscribes to the journal. Tip You can skip this setting if you are not part of an organization or if your organization does not use EZproxy. Wispar does not require institutional access and will still fully work without an institution set. Setting an institution is only useful to get access to paid full articles.

If you are opening the app for the first time, you will see the institution selection screen: Tap the Select my institution button. Choose your organization. Use the search bar to quickly filter and find your institution. Tip If your organization is not in the list, you can skip this step. You can always remove the selected institution by choosing "No institution" at the top of the list.

If you have already completed on-boarding, you can still set your institution through the app settings. From the Home screen: Tap the Wispar icon at the top-right corner and select Settings. Tap the EZProxy tile and choose your institution from the list.
Wispar can be installed on Android devices in three different ways:
https://github.com/Scriptbash/Wispar/releases/latest Tip You can use an app like Obtainium to automatically update the app using the Github releases
For iOS devices, only one installation method is available.

Welcome to the Wispar documentation! Here you’ll find everything you need to install, configure, and use Wispar effectively. This guide is organized into clear sections so you can quickly find answers and instructions, whether you’re a new user or looking for more specific tips.

Installation Methods – Get Wispar set up on your device. Features Overview – Learn how to use Wispar’s key feature. FAQ – Quick answers to frequent questions. Tip If something is unclear or missing, please consider contributing to improve this documentation.

The OpenAlex API allows searches similar to a search engine, making boolean queries possible. Tip OpenAlex supports the use of parentheses in queries. You can group keywords by wrapping them in parentheses across different keyword fields. Use the query preview to confirm that your search is structured correctly.

To search using OpenAlex, first go to the Search screen. Tap the Articles toggle button. Tap the OpenAlex toggle button. To add a keyword, tap the Add keyword button. If more than one keyword is added, boolean operators will appear. In the keyword text field, type in a keyword. To delete a keyword, tap the trash can icon. Tip If multiple words are entered in a single keyword text field, the words will be "ANDed" by OpenAlex. Tap the search button to send your query.
Everything → Searches across titles, abstracts and fulltext Title and abstract → Searches across titles and abstracts Title → Searches using titles only Abstract → Searches using abstracts only
Display name → The title of the paper Cited by count → The number of times a paper was cited by another Publication date → The date of publication
Toggling this option reveals a text field. If a name is entered, the query will be saved and reusable in the library screen. The saved queries can also have their results included the main feed.
